step_by_step_thinking:
Iron deficiency anemia is characterized by decreased hemoglobin levels due to low ferritin stores. This leads to increased Total Iron Binding Capacity (TIBC), as well as reduced transferrin saturation because less iron can bind to available transferrin. Ferritin levels will also be lower reflecting depleted stored iron reserves.

In cases of iron deficiency anemia, we typically see elevated total iron binding capacity (TIBC) since more proteins like transferrin become saturated trying to compensate for the lack of circulating free iron; similarly, transferrin saturation decreases indicating fewer iron molecules being bound despite higher availability. Additionally, serum ferritin concentrations drop significantly when body's iron stores deplete.
